title: transport.rs: build_connector separates TLS config from stream construction — ordering is load-bearing
description: open_authenticated_ws() calls build_connector() before the SOCKS5/TCP connect, with a comment explaining that the connector is applied after the stream is established — TLS must wrap the tunneled stream, not the raw TCP socket. A contributor might move the build_connector() call inside each branch to 'co-locate' it with where it's used. That would be wrong: the connector is built once and passed to ws_upgrade_and_auth; moving it inside would create two separate connectors for the two code paths. The current structure (build once, pass down) is architecturally correct. The comment is load-bearing documentation — do not remove it.
